name: Castroviejo and Yalde Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Yalde – circular desde Castroviejo
description: This difficult 19.9-mile (32.1 km) touring cycling route circles the scenic Yalde Reservoir from Castroviejo, gaining 1648 feet (502 metres)
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Castroviejo traverse a landscape characterized by unique erosion-sculpted rock formations and extensive pine forests. The region features varied terrain, including forest tracks and roads that wind through natural scenery, offering panoramic viewpoints such as Mirador de Castroviejo. Near the source of the Duero River, the area presents a mix of ascents and descents suitable for touring cyclists. These routes often provide views of the Sierra de Urbión and surrounding valleys. Best touring cycling routes around Castroviejo The most popular touring cycling route is Castroviejo and Yalde Reservoir Loop, a 19.9 miles (32.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the distinctive rock formations of Castroviejo and passes by the Yalde Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Moncalvillo Water Trough loop from Sojuela, a difficult 11.0 miles (17.8 km) path. This route offers a challenging loop through the Moncalvillo area, starting and ending in Sojuela. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Sorzano, a 18.1 miles (29.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain around Sorzano, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Touring cycling around Castroviejo is defined by erosion-sculpted rock formations, extensive pine forests, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists, with routes featuring significant elevation changes. The routes in Castroviejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 290 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Castroviejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Castroviejo?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes around Castroviejo, offering a variety of experiences through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Castroviejo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Castroviejo are predominantly challenging, with 9 out of 10 routes rated as difficult. There is one moderate route available, but no easy routes are listed, indicating the area is best suited for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Castroviejo?
A: You can expect varied terrain, including forest tracks and roads that wind through extensive pine forests and unique erosion-sculpted rock formations. Routes often feature significant ascents and descents, providing a challenging yet picturesque experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Castroviejo?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995657084/castroviejo-and-yalde-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castroviejo and Yalde Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option. Other circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31777083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncalvillo Water Trough loop from Sojuela&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33844977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Sorzano&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Which touring cycling routes offer panoramic views?
A: Many routes in the region provide stunning vistas. The area is known for panoramic viewpoints like the Mirador de Castroviejo, which offers breathtaking 360-degree views of the Sierra de Urbión and vast pine forests. Routes often traverse areas with views of Pico Urbión and Pico de las Tres Provincias.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while touring cycling in Castroviejo?
A: The region is famous for its 'magical city' of erosion-sculpted rock formations. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4579884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;LasNeveras&lt;/a&gt;, a viewpoint, or explore the extensive pine forests. The source of the Duero River is also nearby, adding to the natural significance of the area.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Castroviejo?
A: The routes in Castroviejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique geological formations, the serene pine forests, and the challenging yet rewarding ascents and descents that define the region's cycling experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Castroviejo?
A: Given that 9 out of 10 routes are rated as difficult and there are no easy routes listed, Castroviejo's touring cycling options are generally more suited for experienced cyclists rather than families with young children or beginners. The terrain often involves significant elevation changes.

Q: Is Castroviejo suitable for dog-friendly touring cycling?
A: While the natural environment of Castroviejo, with its extensive forests and trails, might seem appealing for dogs, the challenging nature of the touring cycling routes (mostly difficult) means they might not be ideal for all dogs, especially on longer rides or routes with steep climbs. Always consider your dog's fitness and the specific route conditions.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Castroviejo?
A: The region's characteristics, including its pine forests and potential for snow or strong water flow at Cueva Serena, suggest that spring and autumn might offer the most pleasant cycling conditions, avoiding extreme summer heat or winter cold and ice. During rainy seasons or winter, some areas like Cueva Serena are particularly impressive due to water flow or freezing.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the 'Fountain of Life – Moncalvillo Trail loop from Sojuela'?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of Life – Moncalvillo Trail loop from Sojuela&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the Moncalvillo area. While specific landmarks directly on this route are not detailed, the broader Moncalvillo region features highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4067711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Moncalvillo&lt;/a&gt;, a prominent summit, and scenic climbs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7395319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Moncalvillo / Serradero Summit from Navarrete&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Castroviejo?
A: Access to Castroviejo from Duruelo de la Sierra involves a forest track, partly asphalted, and there is a main parking area near the Cueva Serena. This suggests that parking is available at key access points to the natural sites and trailheads, making it convenient for cyclists arriving by car.
tips: The Yalde Reservoir is located on the outskirts of Castroviejo. Built over the course of the Yalde River, a tributary of the Najerilla, the reservoir regulates river flow and supplies water to 11 municipalities located in the course.

Small and quiet town located in the valley of the Yalde river, in the Rioja Alta, with a beautiful wooded environment that surrounds the entire population. The town is located at an altitude of 967 meters, near the Yalde reservoir. It is believed that Castroviejo played an important role as a supplier of fuel from firewood, as well as supplying clay and water.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Yalde Reservoir circular route?
faqs.answer: The estimated cycling time for this 32.1 km route is around 2 hours and 21 minutes. This duration assumes a brisk pace and a very good level of fitness due to the route's challenging nature.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as 'difficult' and requires a very good level of fitness. It features significant elevation changes and varied terrain, making it more suitable for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Yalde Reservoir circular route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain. You'll encounter approximately 18.3 km of asphalt and 5.75 km of paved surfaces, alongside 5.52 km of unpaved sections and 2.56 km of compacted gravel. The path types include roads, designated paths, state roads, and streets, suggesting that a mountain, hybrid, or gravel bike would be most appropriate.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque natural surroundings centered around the Yalde Reservoir. The route also passes through the beautiful wooded environment of the Yalde river valley, starting from the quiet town of Castroviejo.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The primary points of interest are the scenic Yalde Reservoir itself and the charming, quiet town of Castroviejo, which serves as the starting point and is nestled in a beautiful wooded environment.

faqs.question: Where can I park when cycling the Yalde Reservoir circular route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Castroviejo. As a small, quiet town, you should be able to find parking within the settlement to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail research does not specify an ideal time of year. However, given its location in La Rioja, Spain, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential winter conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route around the Yalde Reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the Yalde Reservoir circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily designed for touring cycling. While dogs might be allowed in some areas, it's generally not recommended for dogs to accompany cyclists on a challenging 32.1 km ride with varied terrain and elevation changes. If you do bring a dog, ensure it's well-trained, on a leash, and capable of handling the distance and surfaces, or consider alternative, shorter routes.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including Camiño Francés (Rutas alternativas), Antigua carretera a Castroviejo, and Cno de Castroviejo al Serradero.

faqs.question: What type of bicycle is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mix of paved, unpaved, and compacted gravel surfaces, a mountain bike, hybrid bike, or gravel bike would be best suited for this route. A road bike would likely struggle on the unpaved and gravel sections.
